title: Unidentified Executor-class Star Dreadnought

A prototype [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] '''''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought''' was fitted with multiple [[Fondor Cloaking Device|cloaking devices]] and underwent construction over [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].

==Characteristics==
The ship had several similar characteristics to the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]], although there were some differences: In addition to being equipped with the [[Fondor Cloaking Device|cloaking devices]], various areas of the ship were also left exposed that weren't regularly open-areas, such as near the stern of the ship and at the bow. In addition, from the presence of multiple red horizontal floor girders in the ship, there were also plans to narrow down and separate various trenches. Because the cloaking devices were exposed, they were vulnerable targets. However, this was largely compensated with several [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] turret batteries surrounding the area to outright remove the chance of an aerial bombardment from [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and/or capital ships, as well as mitigate the chances of a starfighter commencing a [[Trench Run Defense|strafing run]] to destroy the cloaking devices.<ref name="SW:RSIII:RS" />

Like the ''[[Terror]]'',<ref name="RA II">''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]''</ref> it also possessed three cloaking devices that allowed the ship to become invisible. One was located close to the bow of the ship; the second was located directly below the ship's command tower; and the third was located at the stern of the ship. The cloaking devices, however, were connected to the power generator, which makes them a crippling weakness to the ship should all of them be destroyed, resulting in its destruction, as Rogue Squadron demonstrated during its mission.<ref name="SW:RSIII:RS" />

===Armament===
The vessel was armed with a number of turbolaser towers that provided defense during its construction. Though the tower defenses could not prevent an assault on the exposed cloaking devices, they made the gauntlet very dangerous for attacking Rebel fighters. The turrets could easily target starfighters who flew too high above the Dreadnought's surface. Many of the turbolaser turrets were put on towers that were higher than normal.<ref name="SW:RSIII:RS" />

==History==
[[File:Fondor_cloaking_device.jpg|thumb|left|150px|One of the [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking devices]] fitted onto the vessel.]]
Fondor Shipyards, who finished development of the ''Executor'',<ref name="Fondor">''[[Classic Star Wars]]''</ref> developed a variant of the ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought line that was equipped with cloaking technology. The vessel was made as part of a ruse by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to give the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] an [[Fondor shipyard assault|easy victory]] that would increase their daring before the climactic [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="SW:RSIII:RS" />

The [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] that the prototype was berthed in was surrounded by an extensive network of [[Imperial Shield Station|space stations]] fitted with [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generators]].<ref name="SW:RSIII:RS" /> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> an initial Rebel strike team blasted their way through one of the stations, with an unmanned [[Unidentified GR-75 medium transport (Fondor)|GR-75 medium transport]] [[Ramming/Legends|ramming]] the dock's primary control center.<ref name="SW:RSIII:RS" />

Having fought their way through the protective shielding system, the Rebel fighters, led by [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], managed to knock out three main cloaking devices on the unfinished<ref name="SW:RSIII:RS" /> [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]],<ref name="EGW" /> which caused it to explode.<ref name="SW:RSIII:RS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought with the cloaking devices appears in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''.
